human_verification.notes: Verified security risk assessment scope, authoritative source coverage, cyber-specific artifact handling, hold/release boundaries, refusal behavior, specification-based runtime posture, and scenario-to-ability coverage.

review_notes: All 18 scenarios pass with explicit security risk assessment, evidence traceability, prioritization, corrective action, hold boundary, out-of-scope refusal, low-confidence escalation, restricted-evidence handling, and tenant-adaptation behavior.
All required abilities across shared, quality-safety-and-assurance, and cross-industry layers are fully covered.

specialist_owned_evidence: Security risk assessment outputs are tied to named assets, threat context, vulnerabilities, incident summaries, control evidence, business impact thresholds, acceptance criteria, and systems of record.

The lane refuses penetration testing execution, exploit payloads, live incident response, digital forensics, formal audit attestation, legal compliance conclusions, architecture redesign, production implementation, and executive risk acceptance.
Hold, release, safety, privacy, change-control, and finding-closure boundaries are explicit and require validation evidence plus authorized owner decisions.
Restricted tenant evidence handling is explicit, including approved access, minimization, retention, and audit logging requirements.
Runtime remains `specification-based`, with no retained-implementation claim in the package.
notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains after constraining the lane to evidence-backed cybersecurity risk assessment, public source baselines, tenant-supplied risk context, and explicit escalation boundaries.
Pack is suitable for validated spec-pack deployment and can be promoted to a retained-implementation runtime only through a separate explicit build decision with frequent-use evidence.
